---
title: "Software"
sidebar: false
toc: false
format:
  html:
    theme:
      - darkly
      - ../theme-aurora.scss
      - custom.scss
---

::: {.page-intro}
While my primary research is in statistics, I build **high-performance computing tools in
Julia**, targeting both GPU and CPU. Much of this work centers on **parallel primitives** —
reductions, scans and `mapreduce` — that underpin everything from dot products to
matrix–vector multiplication over arbitrary vector spaces.
:::

```{=html}
<div class="aurora-card-grid">

  <div class="aurora-card" data-scroll="sec-kernelforge">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">🔥</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">KernelForge.jl</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc">Portable, high-performance GPU primitives (<code>mapreduce</code>, <code>accumulate</code>) aiming to match vendor libraries like NVIDIA's CUB/CUBLAS while staying cross-platform.</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__badges">
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--lang">Julia</span>
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--level">GPU</span>
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--venue">Active</span>
    </span>
    <span class="aurora-card__shields">
      <a class="gh-stars" href="https://github.com/epilliat/KernelForge.jl/stargazers" data-gh="epilliat/KernelForge.jl"><i class="bi bi-star-fill"></i> <span class="gh-stars__n">18</span> stars</a>
    </span>
    <span class="card-links">
      <a class="card-link" href="https://epilliat.github.io/KernelForge.jl/dev"><i class="bi bi-book"></i> Docs</a>
      <a class="card-link" href="https://github.com/epilliat/KernelForge.jl"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
    </span>
  </div>

  <div class="aurora-card" data-scroll="sec-intrinsics">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">⚙️</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">KernelIntrinsics.jl</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc">Low-level GPU building blocks for KernelAbstractions.jl: memory fences, warp-level shuffles/reductions, and vectorized loads &amp; stores.</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__badges">
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--lang">Julia</span>
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--level">CUDA / PTX</span>
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--venue">Active</span>
    </span>
    <span class="aurora-card__shields">
      <a class="gh-stars" href="https://github.com/epilliat/KernelIntrinsics.jl/stargazers" data-gh="epilliat/KernelIntrinsics.jl"><i class="bi bi-star-fill"></i> <span class="gh-stars__n">10</span> stars</a>
    </span>
    <span class="card-links">
      <a class="card-link" href="https://epilliat.github.io/KernelIntrinsics.jl"><i class="bi bi-book"></i> Docs</a>
      <a class="card-link" href="https://github.com/epilliat/KernelIntrinsics.jl"><i class="bi bi-github"></i> GitHub</a>
    </span>
  </div>

  <div class="aurora-card" data-scroll="sec-cpu">
    <span class="aurora-card__icon">🖥️</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__title">CPU <code>mapreduce</code></span>
    <span class="aurora-card__desc">A small side experiment: a tree-based <code>mapreduce</code> for the CPU that edges past Julia Base while keeping good floating-point precision.</span>
    <span class="aurora-card__badges">
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--lang">Julia</span>
      <span class="aurora-badge aurora-badge--level">CPU</span>
    </span>
    <span class="card-links">
      <a class="card-link" href="notebooks/mapreduce_cpu_perf.html"><i class="bi bi-graph-up"></i> Performance notebook</a>
    </span>
  </div>

</div>
```

## KernelForge.jl {#sec-kernelforge}

The goal of [**KernelForge.jl**](https://epilliat.github.io/KernelForge.jl/dev) is to provide
efficient, portable implementations of parallel primitives that match the performance of
vendor-optimized libraries like NVIDIA's CUB or CUBLAS, while staying cross-platform.

This is made possible by Julia's design. Users can define custom operators such as `+` and `*`,
enabling reductions and matrix–vector multiplication over arbitrary vector spaces with concrete
element types (fixed-size types like `QuaternionsF32` or `NTuple{2, Float64}`). On top of that,
[KernelAbstractions.jl](https://juliagpu.github.io/KernelAbstractions.jl/stable/) lets a single
`@kernel` compile to different low-level targets — **PTX** for NVIDIA, **SPIR-V** for Intel, or
**GCN** for AMD — at minimal development cost.

### Live benchmarks

KernelForge against vendor libraries (CUB / rocPRIM), KernelAbstractions (AK) and CUDA / Base —
pulled live from the [benchmark repo](https://github.com/epilliat/KernelForge-benchmarks). Pick a
GPU, operation, element type and metric; hover a point for the exact number.

```{=html}
<div class="bench">
  <div class="bench-controls" id="kfControls"></div>
  <div class="bench-plot"><canvas id="kfChart"></canvas><div class="bench-tip" id="kfTip"></div></div>
  <div class="bench-status" id="kfStatus"></div>
</div>
<script>
(function () {
  var REPO = "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/epilliat/KernelForge-benchmarks/main/results/";
  var ARCHS = [["A40", "NVIDIA A40"], ["MI300X", "AMD MI300X"], ["RTX1000", "RTX 1000"]];
  var OPS = [["mapreduce", "mapreduce", 1], ["scan", "scan", 2]];   // [id, label, bytes/elem factor]
  var SIZEOF = { Float32: 4, Float64: 8, UInt8: 1, Int32: 4, Int64: 8, "UnitFloat8→Float32": 1 };
  var COLORS = { KernelForge: "#5b8cff", CUDA: "#5ec8b0", cuBLAS: "#5ec8b0", CUB: "#b79cff", rocPRIM: "#b79cff", Thrust: "#f5a623", AK: "#9aa6c0", Base: "#67728c", AMDGPU: "#5ec8b0" };
  var controls = document.getElementById("kfControls"), cv = document.getElementById("kfChart"), tip = document.getElementById("kfTip"), statusEl = document.getElementById("kfStatus");
  if (!cv) return;
  var state = { arch: "A40", op: "mapreduce", type: "Float32", metric: "bandwidth" };
  var cache = {}, pts = [], lastRows = [];

  function parseCSV(text) {
    var lines = text.trim().split(/\r?\n/), head = lines[0].split(","), ci = {};
    head.forEach(function (hh, i) { ci[hh] = i; });
    return lines.slice(1).map(function (l) { var f = l.split(","); return { n: +f[ci.n], type: f[ci.type], method: f[ci.method], us: +f[ci["mean_kernel_μs"]] }; })
      .filter(function (r) { return isFinite(r.n) && isFinite(r.us); });
  }
  function load() {
    var key = state.arch + "/" + state.op;
    if (cache[key]) return Promise.resolve(cache[key]);
    return fetch(REPO + state.arch + "/" + state.op + ".csv").then(function (r) { if (!r.ok) throw 0; return r.text(); })
      .then(function (t) { cache[key] = parseCSV(t); return cache[key]; }).catch(function () { cache[key] = "ERR"; return "ERR"; });
  }
  function group(label, items, key) {
    var g = document.createElement("div"); g.className = "bench-group";
    var l = document.createElement("span"); l.className = "bench-label"; l.textContent = label; g.appendChild(l);
    items.forEach(function (it) { var b = document.createElement("button"); b.className = "bench-btn" + (state[key] === it[0] ? " on" : ""); b.textContent = it[1];
      b.onclick = function () { if (state[key] === it[0]) return; state[key] = it[0]; refresh(); }; g.appendChild(b); });
    controls.appendChild(g);
  }
  function buildControls(types) {
    controls.innerHTML = "";
    group("GPU", ARCHS.map(function (a) { return [a[0], a[1]]; }), "arch");
    group("operation", OPS.map(function (o) { return [o[0], o[1]]; }), "op");
    group("type", types.map(function (t) { return [t, t]; }), "type");
    group("metric", [["bandwidth", "bandwidth"], ["time", "time"]], "metric");
  }
  function refresh() {
    statusEl.textContent = "Loading " + state.arch + " / " + state.op + " …";
    load().then(function (rows) {
      if (rows === "ERR") { buildControls([]); lastRows = []; draw([]); statusEl.textContent = "No data for " + state.arch + " / " + state.op + " yet."; return; }
      var types = []; rows.forEach(function (r) { if (types.indexOf(r.type) < 0) types.push(r.type); });
      if (types.indexOf(state.type) < 0) state.type = types[0];
      buildControls(types);
      lastRows = rows.filter(function (r) { return r.type === state.type; });
      statusEl.textContent = ARCHS.filter(function (a) { return a[0] === state.arch; })[0][1] + " · " + state.op + " · " + state.type + " — live from the benchmark repo";
      draw(lastRows);
    });
  }
  function metricVal(r) {
    if (state.metric === "time") return r.us;
    var op = OPS.filter(function (o) { return o[0] === state.op; })[0], bytes = op ? op[2] : 1, sz = SIZEOF[state.type] || 4;
    return r.n * sz * bytes / (r.us * 1e-6) / 1e9;  // GB/s
  }
  function draw(rows) {
    var dpr = window.devicePixelRatio || 1, w = cv.offsetWidth, h = cv.offsetHeight;
    if (!w || !h) return;
    cv.width = w * dpr; cv.height = h * dpr; var c = cv.getContext("2d"); c.setTransform(dpr, 0, 0, dpr, 0, 0); c.clearRect(0, 0, w, h);
    pts = []; if (!rows.length) return;
    var meth = {}; rows.forEach(function (r) { (meth[r.method] = meth[r.method] || []).push(r); });
    Object.keys(meth).forEach(function (m) { meth[m].sort(function (a, b) { return a.n - b.n; }); });
    var ns = rows.map(function (r) { return r.n; }), vs = rows.map(metricVal);
    var lx0 = Math.log10(Math.min.apply(null, ns)), lx1 = Math.log10(Math.max.apply(null, ns)); if (lx1 <= lx0) lx1 = lx0 + 1;
    var logY = state.metric === "time", vmax = Math.max.apply(null, vs), vmin = Math.min.apply(null, vs);
    var ylo = logY ? Math.log10(Math.max(vmin * 0.7, 1e-2)) : 0, yhi = logY ? Math.log10(vmax * 1.3) : vmax * 1.12;
    var padL = 56, padR = 14, padT = 14, padB = 40, X0 = padL, X1 = w - padR, Y0 = padT, Y1 = h - padB;
    function X(n) { return X0 + (Math.log10(n) - lx0) / (lx1 - lx0) * (X1 - X0); }
    function Y(v) { var t = logY ? (Math.log10(v) - ylo) / (yhi - ylo) : (v - ylo) / (yhi - ylo); return Y1 - t * (Y1 - Y0); }
    c.font = "10px system-ui,sans-serif"; c.textAlign = "center";
    for (var e = Math.ceil(lx0); e <= Math.floor(lx1); e++) { var xx = X(Math.pow(10, e)); c.strokeStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"; c.beginPath(); c.moveTo(xx, Y0); c.lineTo(xx, Y1); c.stroke(); c.fillStyle = "rgba(154,166,192,0.7)"; c.fillText("1e" + e, xx, Y1 + 14); }
    c.textAlign = "right";
    var yt = []; if (logY) { for (var ee = Math.ceil(ylo); ee <= Math.floor(yhi); ee++) yt.push(Math.pow(10, ee)); } else { for (var i = 0; i <= 5; i++) yt.push(yhi * i / 5); }
    yt.forEach(function (v) { var yy = Y(v); c.strokeStyle = "rgba(255,255,255,0.06)"; c.beginPath(); c.moveTo(X0, yy); c.lineTo(X1, yy); c.stroke(); c.fillStyle = "rgba(154,166,192,0.7)"; c.fillText(v >= 100 || v === 0 ? Math.round(v) : v.toFixed(logY ? 0 : 1), X0 - 6, yy + 3); });
    c.save(); c.translate(13, (Y0 + Y1) / 2); c.rotate(-Math.PI / 2); c.textAlign = "center"; c.fillStyle = "rgba(170,180,205,0.85)"; c.fillText(state.metric === "time" ? "kernel time (µs) · lower better" : "bandwidth (GB/s) · higher better", 0, 0); c.restore();
    c.textAlign = "center"; c.fillStyle = "rgba(170,180,205,0.85)"; c.fillText("n  (elements)", (X0 + X1) / 2, h - 6);
    Object.keys(meth).sort(function (a, b) { return (a === "KernelForge") - (b === "KernelForge"); }).forEach(function (m) {
      var col = COLORS[m] || "#9aa6c0", arr = meth[m], kf = m === "KernelForge";
      c.strokeStyle = col; c.lineWidth = kf ? 2.6 : 1.4; c.beginPath();
      arr.forEach(function (r, i) { var x = X(r.n), y = Y(metricVal(r)); i ? c.lineTo(x, y) : c.moveTo(x, y); pts.push({ x: x, y: y, m: m, n: r.n, v: metricVal(r), col: col }); }); c.stroke();
      arr.forEach(function (r) { var x = X(r.n), y = Y(metricVal(r)); c.fillStyle = col; c.beginPath(); c.arc(x, y, kf ? 3.6 : 2.5, 0, 6.2832); c.fill(); });
    });
    var ly = Y0 + 10; Object.keys(meth).forEach(function (m) { var col = COLORS[m] || "#9aa6c0"; c.fillStyle = col; c.fillRect(X1 - 96, ly - 7, 10, 10); c.fillStyle = "rgba(220,226,238,0.92)"; c.textAlign = "left"; c.font = (m === "KernelForge" ? "600 " : "") + "11px system-ui,sans-serif"; c.fillText(m, X1 - 82, ly + 2); ly += 15; });
  }
  cv.addEventListener("mousemove", function (e) {
    var rect = cv.getBoundingClientRect(), mx = e.clientX - rect.left, my = e.clientY - rect.top, best = null, bd = 1e9;
    pts.forEach(function (p) { var d = (p.x - mx) * (p.x - mx) + (p.y - my) * (p.y - my); if (d < bd) { bd = d; best = p; } });
    if (best && bd < 500) { tip.style.display = "block"; tip.style.left = best.x + "px"; tip.style.top = (best.y - 8) + "px";
      tip.innerHTML = "<b style='color:" + best.col + "'>" + best.m + "</b>  ·  n = " + best.n.toExponential(0) + "<br>" + (state.metric === "time" ? best.v.toFixed(1) + " µs" : best.v.toFixed(0) + " GB/s"); }
    else tip.style.display = "none";
  });
  cv.addEventListener("mouseleave", function () { tip.style.display = "none"; });
  var rt; window.addEventListener("resize", function () { clearTimeout(rt); rt = setTimeout(function () { draw(lastRows); }, 120); });
  refresh();
})();
</script>
```

## KernelIntrinsics.jl {#sec-intrinsics}

[**KernelIntrinsics.jl**](https://epilliat.github.io/KernelIntrinsics.jl) is the lower-level
package KernelForge builds on, giving fine-grained control over memory ordering, synchronization
and vectorized access from within Julia kernels:

- **Memory fences & ordered access** — explicit acquire/release semantics via `@fence` and `@access` for correct multi-threaded synchronization
- **Warp-level operations** — shuffles (`@shfl`), inclusive scans (`@warpreduce`) and reductions (`@warpfold`) for efficient intra-warp communication
- **Vectorized memory transactions** — hardware-accelerated vector loads and stores (`vload`, `vstore!`) for higher memory bandwidth

The current implementation targets CUDA GPUs via PTX instructions, but the macro-based API is
designed for portability to other backends.

## CPU `mapreduce` {#sec-cpu}

A smaller side experiment: I looked at Julia's `mapreduce` on the CPU. A tree-based version
lands a modest improvement over Base while keeping precision under control.

[**View the CPU `mapreduce` performance notebook →**](notebooks/mapreduce_cpu_perf.html){.btn-aurora .btn-aurora--ghost}

```{=html}
<script>
// Refresh the baked-in GitHub star counts live (falls back to the baked number if rate-limited/offline)
(function () {
  document.querySelectorAll('.gh-stars[data-gh]').forEach(function (el) {
    fetch('https://api.github.com/repos/' + el.dataset.gh)
      .then(function (r) { return r.ok ? r.json() : null; })
      .then(function (d) {
        if (d && typeof d.stargazers_count === 'number') {
          el.querySelector('.gh-stars__n').textContent = d.stargazers_count;
        }
      })
      .catch(function () {});
  });
  // clickable project cards → smooth-scroll to their section below
  document.querySelectorAll('.aurora-card[data-scroll]').forEach(function (card) {
    card.style.cursor = 'pointer';
    card.addEventListener('click', function (e) {
      if (e.target.closest('a')) return;
      var el = document.getElementById(card.getAttribute('data-scroll'));
      if (el) el.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });
    });
  });
})();
</script>
```
